highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.126 Released
highlights.summary: The latest release adds a `/model` picker that lists models from your gateway's `/v1/models` endpoint when using a custom `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`, making it easier to switch models with compatible gateways. It also introduces `claude project purge [path]` to cleanly delete all Claude Code state for a project, including transcripts, tasks, file history, and config entries. This is useful for resetting a project workspace or freeing up storage.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.121 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds an `alwaysLoad` option to MCP server config so that specific tools are always available without going through tool-search deferral — handy for tools you rely on constantly. It also adds `claude plugin prune` to remove orphaned plugin dependencies, keeping your environment tidy. A new type-to-filter feature was also introduced to make navigating options faster.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest addition to the Opus model family. This is significant for Claude Code users as newer Opus models typically bring improved reasoning and coding capabilities. Check Anthropic's blog for details on what's new and how to access it through Claude Code.

highlights.title: FULL Claude Code Tutorial for Beginners in 2026! (Step-By-Step)
highlights.summary: This step-by-step YouTube tutorial is designed for developers who are brand new to Claude Code in 2026, walking through setup and core workflows from scratch. It's a great starting point if you haven't used Claude Code before or want a refresher on the latest features. Bookmark this for onboarding teammates who are just getting started.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 minutes
highlights.summary: This concise YouTube tutorial promises to get you up to speed with Claude Code in just 30 minutes, making it ideal for developers who want a quick but thorough overview. It likely covers key commands, workflows, and tips to help you become productive fast. A solid watch if you prefer a time-boxed learning session over longer tutorials.

highlights.title: Uber torches 2026 AI budget on Claude Code in four months
highlights.summary: Reports suggest Uber burned through its entire 2026 AI budget using Claude Code in just four months, highlighting how quickly costs can escalate with agentic AI coding tools at enterprise scale. This is an important cautionary tale for teams adopting Claude Code without usage guardrails. Consider setting up budget alerts and reviewing your usage policies before rolling out Claude Code broadly.

highlights.title: Who owns the code Claude Code wrote?
highlights.summary: This article explores the legal question of copyright ownership for code generated by Claude Code, a nuanced issue that every developer and company using AI coding tools should understand. The answer may depend on how much human creative input was involved and the jurisdiction you operate in. It's worth reading before shipping AI-generated code in commercial products.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.119 Released
highlights.summary: Settings configured via `/config` (such as theme, editor mode, and verbose output) now persist to `~/.claude/settings.json` and respect the project/local/policy override hierarchy, so your preferences carry across sessions. A new `prUrlTemplate` setting lets you point the footer PR badge to a custom code-review URL instead of github.com, which is great for teams using GitLab or self-hosted review tools. These quality-of-life improvements make Claude Code easier to configure and standardize across a team.

tips_of_the_day: Use `claude project purge [path]` to reset a project's Claude Code state when switching contexts or handing off a repo — it clears transcripts and config entries so the next session starts fresh.

Before rolling out Claude Code to your whole team, set a monthly token budget and enable usage alerts in your Anthropic Console to avoid unexpected runaway costs like those reported at enterprise scale.
Add `alwaysLoad: true` to MCP server configs for tools you use in every session — this bypasses tool-search deferral and makes those tools instantly available without any extra prompting overhead.
